arguments.prompt:
You are working in `/tmp/sv-e87a0bcae997460982e24c387d0e5866/UniversityCourseCatalog`, a freshly scaffolded .NET 11 Blazor Web App with static SSR (no interactivity mode). Bootstrap is included. The project uses the default sidebar layout.

Build a university course catalog app with these requirements:

## Data Model & Service
Create `Models/` and `Services/` directories.

### Models/Department.cs
```csharp
namespace UniversityCourseCatalog.Models;

public class Department
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = "";
    public string Name { get; set; } = "";
    public string Description { get; set; } = "";
}
```

### Models/Instructor.cs
```csharp
namespace UniversityCourseCatalog.Models;

public class Instructor
{
    public string Name { get; set; } = "";
    public string Title { get; set; } = "";
    public string Bio { get; set; } = "";
    public string Email { get; set; } = "";
}
```

### Models/Course.cs
```csharp
namespace UniversityCourseCatalog.Models;

public class Course
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = "";
    public string Code { get; set; } = "";
    public string Title { get; set; } = "";
    public string Description { get; set; } = "";
    public int Credits { get; set; }
    public string Schedule { get; set; } = "";
    public string DepartmentId { get; set; } = "";
    public Instructor Instructor { get; set; } = new();
    public bool IsFeatured { get; set; }
}
```

### Models/EnrollmentRequest.cs
```csharp
namespace UniversityCourseCatalog.Models;

public class EnrollmentRequest
{
    public string StudentName { get; set; } = "";
    public string StudentId { get; set; } = "";
    public string CourseId { get; set; } = "";
}
```

### Services/CatalogService.cs
Create an in-memory service with realistic seed data. Include at least 5 departments (Computer Science, Mathematics, English, Physics, History) and at least 3 courses per department (15+ courses total). Each course should have a realistic instructor with a short bio. Mark 4-5 courses as featured.

Methods:
- `IReadOnlyList<Department> GetDepartments()`
- `Department? GetDepartment(string id)`
- `IReadOnlyList<Course> GetCourses(string? departmentId = null, string? searchTerm = null)`
- `Course? GetCourse(string id)`
- `IReadOnlyList<Course> GetFeaturedCourses()`
- `void SubmitEnrollmentRequest(EnrollmentRequest request)` (just stores in a list, no real processing)

Register it as a singleton in Program.cs.

## Pages

### Components/Pages/Home.razor
Replace the existing home page. Show:
- A hero section with university name "Lakewood University" and tagline
- Featured courses section (cards with code, title, credits, link to detail)
- Department links section (list of all departments linking to filtered course list)

### Components/Pages/Courses.razor (route: /courses, /courses/{departmentId})
- Search box (standard form GET submission with query parameter `?search=`)
- Department filter dropdown (standard form GET submission)
- Table or card list of matching courses showing: Code, Title, Department, Credits, Schedule
- Each course links to its detail page

### Components/Pages/CourseDetail.razor (route: /courses/detail/{courseId})
- Full course info: code, title, description, credits, schedule
- Instructor section with name, title, bio, email
- Link back to course list
- Link to enrollment form pre-selecting this course

### Components/Pages/Enroll.razor (route: /enroll)
- Standard HTML form (POST) with fields: Student Name, Student ID, Course Selection (dropdown of all courses)
- If a `courseId` query parameter is provided, pre-select that course
- On successful POST, show a confirmation message
- Use `[SupplyParameterFromForm]` and `[SupplyParameterFromQuery]` for the form binding

### Delete Weather.razor — it's the template sample page.

## Layout Updates

### Components/Layout/NavMenu.razor
Update navigation links:
- Home
- Courses
- Enroll
Remove the Weather link. Change the brand name to "Lakewood University".

### Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor
Keep the default sidebar layout but update the top-row to say "Course Catalog" instead of linking to ASP.NET docs.

## Styling
Update `wwwroot/app.css` — append custom styles for:
- Hero section with a dark blue/navy background (#1a3a5c), white text, padding
- Course cards with subtle shadows and hover effects
- Featured badge styling
- Keep it clean and professional, university-appropriate

## Important Implementation Notes
- This is static SSR only (no @rendermode directives needed — it's the default)
- Forms use standard HTML form submission (GET for search/filter, POST for enrollment)
- Use `[SupplyParameterFromQuery]` for search/filter parameters  
- Use `[SupplyParameterFromForm]` for the enrollment form
- Use `<form method="get">` for search, `<form method="post" @formname="enrollment">` with `<AntiforgeryToken />` for enrollment
- Use `NavigationManager` only if needed, prefer standard links
- All pages should have `<PageTitle>` components

After creating all files, run `dotnet build` to verify everything compiles. Fix any errors.
